compiled_state.title: Sub-agent prompt guidance: mandatory read_first gate before edits
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Context

Sub-agents dispatched by sprint receive a vague instruction to "read relevant existing code" before implementing, which leads to either unfocused context consumption or skipped reads. Meanwhile, the test-writing step duplicates work already done by dedicated RED test writer tasks. By replacing these two steps with precise, bounded instructions — a mandatory read gate tied to the ticket's file list plus exemplar discovery for new files, and a test validation step that reads existing RED tests — agents get exactly the context they need without waste.

## Success Criteria

- Sub-agents read every file listed in their task ticket's file impact section before making any edits
- For files with create action, the agent finds and reads one suffix-matched exemplar (same directory first, then project-wide) to learn local conventions — skipping when no suffix pattern is extractable
- Suffix matching works across PascalCase (*Controller.java), snake_case (*_controller.py), and kebab-case (*-controller.ts) naming conventions
- The test-writing step (current Step 4) is replaced with a test validation step: the agent reads existing RED tests for its task, confirms they exist, and flags missing tests rather than writing new ones
- CHECKPOINT 2/6 records which files and exemplars were read
- The checkpoint sequence remains 6 stages with no renumbering

## Dependencies

None

## Approach

Option A: Modify task-execution.md (the sub-agent dispatch prompt template) with two targeted changes: (1) Replace Step 3 with a precise read_first gate — mandatory reading of the ticket's file list, plus suffix-matched exemplar discovery at agent runtime for create-action files. (2) Replace Step 4 with a test validation step that reads existing RED tests and confirms they exist. No new scripts or tooling — all logic is inline in the template. Suffix extraction handles PascalCase, snake_case, and kebab-case; skips when no pattern is extractable.
